svg.text: Device binding & sandbox spawn — the key-custody & security view
who holds which key · what proves possession · why no single party can forge, bind, or spawn on its own
KEY INVENTORY — where each secret lives & who can see it (colour = trust boundary; the anchor of every step below)
same ceremony for every machine — a device (channel grants at claim, ≥1 required) or a delegate sandbox (in-band claim, no QR)
ESP32 flash OR sandbox · own K10 · signs itself
the only source of truth for authority
generate K10 on the machine (ESP32 flash / sandbox)
secp256k1, signed on-device · D_priv persisted, never leaves
no bearer — endpoint is PoP-gated, so a bad sig writes no row
names NO master · TTL 600s · inert until claimed
owner carries the pairing_code (QR on screen) → master scans
out-of-band, human proximity — the one step no key protects, so show it only to the owner
broker derives O_agent = SHA256(O_master // label); returns device artifact to review
ONE Touch ID (K11) signs the userOpHash
broker only RELAYS /accept/{build,submit} — it never writes chain
binds D_pub→O_agent (tier AGENT, CAP_MINT, k11=0) + scope
then device polls → broker mints J1_agent FRESH (scope now live → cap-mint works)
Ⓑ SANDBOX SPAWN — master authorizes, broker executes
NEVER triggered by device pairing — the default delegate spawns at onboarding, more by option; a device claim only attaches channels (plan §4.4)
a phone never launches cloud infra directly
SCOPED STS, server-side — sandbox holds no long-lived S3 creds
register back: O_agent_A ↔ sandbox ↔ device_key → surfaced in the Master hub
THE TRUST CHAIN — no link can be skipped, each is a different key held by a different party
SECURITY INVARIANTS — what the design guarantees no matter who is compromised
✗ agent never holds K11 — K10-only, no human-presence credential of its own
✗ broker never writes chain — it relays the master's UserOp; authority lands only under the master's key
✗ D_priv (K10) never leaves the device / sandbox — a leaked K10 touches only that one actor's wallet + prefix
✗ pairing request is UNBOUND + inert — Sybil-safe: names no master, does nothing until a master claims the code
✓ cap-token is short-TTL + revocable; workers re-verify scope on-chain every call (§17.5 four-layer)
✓ per-actor omni → own S3 prefix + IAM PrincipalTag; the sandbox reads via a scoped, server-side STS
✓ master = ERC-4337 P-256 account — no client ever custodies a secp256k1 key; gas is paymaster-sponsored
✓ revoke = master setScope → the next cap-mint is refused, then the cap TTL bounds the offline window
